entries.definition:
d–k–k to pull down, to level up, level sand dunes, low banks of mud, short stout horses, humpless camel, to be stout and well built; to fill in a pit with dirt; to weaken, to humiliate. Of this root, four forms occur seven times: دُكَّت dukka twice; دَكّ dakk three times; دَكَّة dakkatan once and دَكَّاء dakkāʾ once.
دُكَّت dukka [pass. of v. دَكَّ dakka, trans.] to be crushed, to be flattened out (69:14) وَحُمِلَتِ الْأَرْضُ وَالْجِبَالُ فَدُكَّتَا دَكَّةً وَاحِدَةً and the earth and the mountains are lifted up and crushed in a single crush.

دَكّ dakk [v. n.] 1 (act of) crushing, levelling up, flattening out, pounded, pounding (89:21) كَلَّا إِذَا دُكَّتِ الْأَرْضُ دَكًّا دَكًّا no indeed!, when the earth is pounded, pounded and [again] pounded 2 [functioning as n.] an even or a level place; land, or ground, broken, and made even, flattened earth (7:143) فَلَمَّا تَجَلَّى رَبُّهُ لِلْجَبَلِ جَعَلَهُ دَكًّا and when his Lord revealed Himself to the mountain, He made it flattened earth.

دَكَّة dakkatan [n. of unit] a crushing (69:14) وَحُمِلَتِ الْأَرْضُ وَالْجِبَالُ فَدُكَّتَا دَكَّةً وَاحِدَةً and the earth and the mountains are lifted up and crushed in a single crushing.

دَكَّاء dakkāʾ [quasi-pass. fem. part.] flattened out, levelled (18:98) فَإِذَا جَاءَ وَعْدُ رَبِّي جَعَلَهُ دَكَّاءَ but when my Lord’s promise is fulfilled, He will turn it [this barrier] into flattened out [ground].
